name: Zero Trust Architecture
owner: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
description: NIST Special Publication 800-207 defines Zero Trust Architecture (ZTA) — a security model centered on resources rather than network segments, with continuous authentication and authorization, least-privilege per-request access, dynamic policy enforcement, and assumption-of-breach posture.
relationToCloudSecurity: Foundational for cloud and hybrid-environment security architectures. Most modern cloud-security designs reference 800-207. CISA's Zero Trust Maturity Model and federal Zero Trust strategy both build on this.
relationToAISecurity: Underlying access-control architecture applies to AI systems and inference endpoints; AI-specific overlays may be added later.

license.notes: Work of NIST is in the US public domain (17 USC §105).

lifecycle.notes: Published 2020-08-11. Current; supplementary practice guidance via NIST SP 1800-35 (NCCoE Implementing a Zero Trust Architecture).

current_extraction.notes: Source PDF marker-extracted to markdown. extract_800_207.py walks the numbered-section headings into per-section structured CSV/JSON: 52 sections across 7 chapters (Introduction, Zero Trust Basics, Logical Components, Deployment Scenarios, Threats, Federal Guidance Interactions, Migration). 800-207 is a narrative guide rather than a control framework — section-level extraction supports cross-referencing but is not per-control structured.

notes: Metadata stub; acquire from NIST CSRC and extract.
